title: rubus macrophyllus subsp. lasiaxon
intro: Rubus macrophyllus subsp. lasiaxon, also known as the Chinese raspberry, is a type of bramble or wild fruit-bearing shrub native to China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus macrophyllus subsp. lasiaxon include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote overall health and well-being.
